parameters.content:
## Contact / feedback → email (kb:feedback-channel)

The in-app contact + feedback form POSTs here, so a submission arrives as an email instead of the user having to open their own mail client. `src/lib/integrations/n8n/contact.ts` does the POST.

**Setup:**
1. Import this workflow, open the Webhook node, copy the **production** URL. Its path must be `reckons-contact` so it matches `POST {n8nBaseUrl}/webhook/reckons-contact`.
2. In Reckons.AI → Settings → Integrations: set the n8n base URL. That alone switches the form from mailto to direct submit — `n8nConfigured()` checks nothing else.
3. Set the Send Email credential. The recipient is already matthew.roe@data-insight.solutions; change it there.

**Reply-To is the submitter**, so replying to the notification reaches the person who wrote in.

Payload: `{ name, email, message, source?, submittedAt }`.

**THIS WEBHOOK IS PUBLIC AND UNAUTHENTICATED.** Anyone who learns the URL can post to it, so treat it as a spam-capable inbox: the Validate node drops empty and oversized messages, but it is not a spam filter. If it gets abused, put n8n behind Cloudflare/rate-limiting or add a shared secret header — the app does not send one today.

nodes.name: Reckons contact webhook
nodes.notes: responseMode onReceived returns 200 immediately, which is what makes the form show '✓ Sent' — the app treats any non-2xx as a failure and tells the user to email instead.

nodes.name: Send feedback email
nodes.notes: Set your SMTP credential. Reply-To is the submitter's address, so hitting reply answers them directly. Swap for the Gmail/Slack/ticket node to change channel — the app does not care how the feedback reaches you.
